$comment: Reminders run every 5 minutes because the bot offers 30-minute and 2-hour
credits, whose reminder lead times are 10 and 30 minutes. A daily cron
cannot deliver those at all: the reminder window opens and closes long
before the next run, and the credit then falls out of the pre-due query.
5 minutes is the coarsest schedule that still lands inside a 10-minute
window. Per-day housekeeping (score decay, subscriptions) stays on its own
daily schedule so it is not re-run 288 times a day.
NOTE: frequent crons require a Vercel Pro plan. On Hobby, Vercel caps cron
to once per day and this schedule will not fire as written — see the
external-scheduler fallback in the reminders route.
